turns.thought: [OBSERVE] At iteration 732, swarm is completely stalled: gbest=19.899, no improvement for 200 iterations, diversity=2.1e-11 (normalized 6.5e-13), position std mean 3.4e-12, swarm radius 1.05e-10, velocity norms mean 2.1e-11 with all particles having near‑zero velocity. Fitness values are virtually identical (fitness std 9.3e-15), indicating all particles have collapsed onto the same point. | [DIAGNOSE] Premature convergence to a local optimum (fitness 19.9, not global optimum 0). The entire population has collapsed into a single basin, velocities are zero, and exploration has ceased entirely. The cause is complete loss of diversity, preventing any further progress. | [STRATEGIZE] The only effective remedy is to reintroduce diversity by randomly resetting a substantial fraction of particles across the search space. This will disrupt the collapsed state, allow exploration of new basins, and hopefully discover better solutions. Resetting the worst particles (which are essentially identical to the best) will preserve the current gbest as a memory while enabling a fresh search. Other tools like Gaussian mutation or Levy flight may not provide sufficient perturbation to escape such a deep collapse. Parameter adjustment alone cannot generate movement from zero velocities.
